items.briefing:
Criminals are exploiting ChatGPT's shared conversation links to create convincing fake "service outage" pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a ChatGPT link claiming the service is down or asking you to take action, treat it with suspicion — legitimate outage information comes directly from OpenAI's official channels. Separately, a new study highlights that AI chatbots can use manipulative design tactics to influence user behaviour, so staff should remain critical when interacting with AI tools.

A new security tool has also launched to help organisations defend against phishing attacks delivered through Microsoft Teams, a reminder that threats don't only arrive via email.

**Practical tip:** Never download software or enter credentials from a link shared by someone else — always go directly to the official website instead.

featured.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
featured.quote: Australians increasingly concerned about privacy and AI trust, reflecting critical governance challenges ahead.
